content.method: Pilot randomized or quasi-experimental trial of alternative admissions criteria (e.g., blind grading, lottery-among-qualified, geographic quotas) in a subset of localities, with pre/post comparison of applicant SES composition and post-admission outcome tracking.
content.question: Can admissions-process reforms be designed to reduce SES-linked bias without simply shifting the selection mechanism to another SES-correlated proxy (e.g., private tutoring, informal preparation networks, or discretionary interview criteria)?
content.why_it_matters: TP-live-t3 is politically framed as the minister's preferred lower-conflict lever, but the evidence on self-exclusion suggests disadvantaged families' non-participation is driven by strategic/behavioral factors rather than exam design alone. If bias merely relocates to a new proxy, this reform would consume political capital without producing measurable equity gains.
